name: Jian Lv
title: Professor
affiliation: International Center of Computational Method and Software, College of Physics, Jilin University
summary: My research focuses on the development and application of the CALYPSO structure prediction method for materials discovery in physics, chemistry, and materials science.
highlights: Develops and applies CALYPSO-based approaches for predicting crystal structures and material properties.
Works at the intersection of condensed matter physics, chemistry, high-pressure science, and materials informatics.
Maintains an academic publication record indexed through Google Scholar.

publications.title: Crystal structure prediction via particle-swarm optimization
publications.authors: Y. Wang, J. Lv, L. Zhu, Y. Ma
publications.note: Early particle-swarm optimization framework for crystal structure prediction.

publications.title: Adaptive genetic algorithm for crystal structure prediction
publications.authors: Y. Wang, J. Lv, L. Zhu, Y. Ma
publications.note: Foundational work related to CALYPSO crystal structure prediction.

publications.title: CALYPSO: A method for crystal structure prediction
publications.authors: Y. Wang, J. Lv, L. Zhu, Y. Ma
publications.note: Software-method paper for predicting stable and metastable crystal structures from composition and external conditions.

publications.title: The CALYPSO methodology for structure prediction
publications.authors: Q. Tong, J. Lv, P. Gao, Y. Wang
publications.note: Review-style overview of the CALYPSO methodology and its use in structure searches and materials discovery.

publications.title: Crystal Structure Prediction via Efficient Sampling of the Potential Energy Surface
publications.authors: Y. Wang, J. Lv, P. Gao, Y. Ma
publications.note: Profile-level review connecting crystal structure prediction, potential energy surfaces, and materials discovery.
